policy.shadow_library_rule: Block if filename or metadata explicitly indicates Anna's Archive, LibGen, Z-Library, EBooksWorld, or another shadow-library/export origin.
policy.notes: This manifest backfills the current local corpus into source_record-shaped entries. It records file identity, relevance, status, safe notes strategy, and existing vault coverage only.

records.title: Applied Machine Learning and AI for Engineers
records.safe_notes_strategy: Canon-ready local-book applied ML route slice. Direct-read synthesis now includes Chapter 14 deepening for managed AI services, OCR route choices, moderation semantics, container/privacy tradeoffs, provider drift, Chapter 4 deepening for sparse text vectorization contracts, Count/TF-IDF/Hashing tradeoffs, Naive Bayes versus Logistic Regression baselines, threshold semantics, sklearn persistence constraints, Chapter 8 deepening for multilayer-perceptron structure, activation-driven nonlinearity, training-versus-inference asymmetry, optimization difficulty, and compute-aware neural-route review, Chapter 9 deepening for TensorFlow/Keras workflow contracts, validation policy, width/depth tradeoffs, output-head/loss alignment, and imbalanced-class evaluation, Chapter 12 canon-ready coverage for detector-output contracts, NMS/IoU threshold and reproducibility behavior, ONNX/runtime decode ownership, and custom object-detection export constraints, Chapter 11 deepening for identity-sensitive face-pipeline design, open-set rejection, and biometric-governance release controls, and Chapter 13 deepening for tokenizer/vectorizer state, sequence-length and masking contracts, retriever-reader QA, translation generation, and pretrained-encoder economics. Store only original notes, not raw book text.

existing_vault_coverage.coverage_summary: Canon-ready applied ML engineering patterns plus Chapter 14 managed-AI deepening for service-boundary/versioning decisions, OCR route selection, moderation semantics, container/privacy posture, provider drift, Chapter 4 text-classification deepening for sparse vectorization contracts, Count/TF-IDF/Hashing tradeoffs, Naive Bayes versus Logistic Regression baselines, threshold semantics, and sklearn pipeline persistence, Chapter 5 SVM deepening for maximum-margin geometry, kernel choice, coupled C/gamma tuning, scaling/pipeline parity, multiclass strategy, and probability-calibration caveats, Chapter 8 deep-learning-foundation deepening for multilayer-perceptron structure, weights/biases, activation-driven nonlinearity, forward propagation, optimization difficulty, and compute-aware neural-route review, Chapter 9 neural-network workflow deepening for TensorFlow/Keras role boundaries, Sequential API topology, compile/fit/predict contracts, validation policy, output-head/loss alignment, and imbalanced-class evaluation, Chapter 12 canon-ready object-detection deepening for detector-output contracts, NMS/IoU threshold behavior, R-CNN-family versus YOLOv3 tradeoffs, ONNX/runtime decode ownership, and custom-detector export constraints, Chapter 11 face-pipeline deepening for detector choice, crop/alignment, face-specific transfer learning, open-set identity handling, and biometric-governance constraints, and Chapter 13 NLP deepening for tokenizer/vectorizer state, sequence-length and masking contracts, embedding choice, QA/translation route structure, and pretrained-encoder economics.
